name: sdlc-workflow
description: Complete SDLC workflow with integrated code review, unified design system, Diátaxis documentation, analysis, observability, and git lifecycle. 32 lifecycle commands (10 stages + wf-design [22 sub-commands] + wf-next + wf-status + wf-resume + wf-sync + wf-announce + wf-amend + wf-extend + wf-ideate + wf-how + wf-quick + wf-rca + wf-investigate + wf-discover + wf-instrument + wf-experiment + wf-benchmark + wf-profile + wf-skip + wf-close) with branch-aware git operations, wf-validate pre-write hook, 31 review commands, 7 aggregate reviews, 15 skills (design dispatcher + imagegen + 4 analysis + 7 Diátaxis + wf-profile), and setup-wide-logging command. wf-how routes across five modes: quick code answers, codebase exploration (parallel Explore agents + synthesis), deep web research (6–8 agents, 200+ sources), workflow artifact explanation, and findings explanation — with optional Diátaxis output. wf-plan includes reuse scan: Explore sub-agent 1 searches the wider codebase for existing utilities and capabilities before planning new implementations. v8.14: four standalone workflow entry points — wf-hotfix, wf-update-deps, wf-docs, wf-refactor. v8.17: wf-quick — compressed planning workflow. v8.18: wf-rca — read-only root-cause analysis with three parallel diagnosis sub-agents. v8.19: wf-investigate — investment discovery; parallel ROI-ranked candidate survey. v8.20: wf-discover — problem validation; external competitor/user/market research, build/do-not-build recommendation. v8.21: wf-instrument — observability augmentation; dark path detection + signal design, writes 04b-instrument.md. v8.22: wf-experiment — experiment design augmentation; hypothesis extraction, A-B/flag/canary design with metrics and rollback criteria, writes 04c-experiment.md. v8.23: wf-benchmark — two-mode perf wrapper; baseline before implement, compare after; regression tripwires at >10% CPU / >25% memory. v8.24: wf-profile skill + command — static + dynamic profiling via Node --cpu-prof, Go pprof, py-spy; standalone artifact at .ai/profiles/<run-id>/. v8.25: wf-skip — skip a stage without running it; stub artifact keeps downstream prerequisites satisfied. v8.26: wf-close — archive a workflow at any stage; five close reasons; 99-close.md with completed/incomplete summary and manual cleanup checklist. v8.27: Unified wf-design — 14 standalone design-* skills and 5 wf-design* commands collapsed into a single wf-design command (22 sub-commands) backed by a skills/design/ dispatcher + 24 reference docs. New imagegen skill with capability waterfall (built-in image_gen → gpt-image-2 codex exec → nano-banana-pro Gemini API → text fallback). Imagegen probes in shape and craft sub-commands. Parallel codebase inspection sub-agents (token scanner, framework detector, context loader, surface ranger) before design work. Register-aware brand vs product divergence in all reference docs. Fixed: colorize side-stripe border anti-pattern, outdated animation-avoidance stance in animate/optimize/polish. v8.28: SDLC-aware sub-command routing for wf-design. craft splits by mode (workflow → 02c-craft.md visual contract → routes to /wf-implement; freestanding → writes code). Stage-gated sub-commands: audit/critique require implement-or-later; 15 transformation sub-commands cannot run before implement. Transformations register as augmentations and write design-notes/<sub>-<timestamp>.md. Mutation gate distinguishes code/artifact/context/read-only types. v8.29: Closed the artifact-consumption gap — post-design stages (wf-slice, wf-plan, wf-implement, wf-verify, wf-review, wf-handoff, wf-ship, wf-retro) now consume design artifacts (02b-design.md, 02c-craft.md, design-notes/*, 07-design-audit.md, 07-design-critique.md). All stages that read augmentations: list now handle every type (instrument/experiment/benchmark/design-*) with type-specific behavior: implement adds instrumentation signals + experiment wiring as it codes; verify re-runs type-specific checks (axe-core for harden, perf for optimize, cohort tests for experiment, compare-mode for benchmark); handoff/ship translate every augmentation type to user-language changelog entries. Visual contract enforcement: 05-implement-<slice>.md gains a § Visual Contract Honored section listing per-fidelity-item disposition; 06-verify-<slice>.md gains a § Augmentation Verification section with per-augmentation pass/regression. Workflow-type awareness added to wf-implement/wf-verify/wf-review: compressed mode (workflow-type: quick → reads 01-quick.md) and forwarded mode (workflow-type: rca/investigate → reads 01-rca.md/01-investigate.md as rich context behind the synthesized 02-shape.md). Fixes a critical broken contract where wf-quick produced 01-quick.md but wf-implement only knew how to read 02-shape.md + 04-plan.md. v8.30: Per-slice review artifacts. `wf-review` now writes `07-review-<slice-slug>.md` (master verdict per slice) and `07-review-<slice-slug>-<command>.md` (per-command sub-reviews) instead of a single workflow-wide `07-review.md`/`07-review-<command>.md`. Running review on a second slice no longer overwrites the first slice's review — every reviewed slice keeps its own master verdict and per-command findings on disk, making 07 consistent with the per-slice naming already used by `03-slice-<slug>.md` through `06-verify-<slug>.md`. `wf-handoff` now parses each per-slice review's `verdict` and `metric-findings-blocker` frontmatter and refuses to proceed if any slice in scope has unresolved blockers, listing the offending slice slug(s). Consumers updated: `wf-implement` reviews mode (slice-scoped, accepts explicit slice arg); `wf-amend from-review` (per-slice, aggregates siblings on cross-slice spec errors); `wf-extend from-review` (globs every per-slice review since missing-capability findings often span slices); `wf-retro`, `wf-ship`, `wf-how findings` (glob across all per-slice review files); `wf-skip review` (slice-scoped stub); `wf-status` and `wf-verify` frontmatter `refs.review` updated; `wf-handoff` and `wf-ship` frontmatter changed `refs.review` to `refs.reviews: [...]`. v8.30.1: wf-implement now loads design references named in `02b-design.md`'s YAML frontmatter `recommended-references: [...]` array and reads each as `skills/design/reference/<name>.md` for read-only implementation rationale. Closes a wiring gap from v8.29 where the brief's recommended-references section was produced by `/wf-design shape` but never consumed downstream. Fixes typo in `reference/shape.md` (`typography.md` → `typeset.md`) and mandates frontmatter mirror so the array is machine-parseable.
